summary: The chart image is a ring chart, also known as a donut chart, titled "Net Savings (% of GNI) – Share by Scenario (2006-2029)." This chart visually represents the distribution of net savings as a percentage of Gross National Income (GNI) across different scenarios over the period from 2006 to 2029. The chart is divided into five distinct segments, each representing a different scenario, and each segment is color-coded for easy differentiation. Starting from the top and moving clockwise, the first segment, colored in a light green, represents the "Policy-Adjusted (90% Nat.)" scenario, which accounts for 27.2% of the net savings. Next, the "Excluding Region" scenario, shown in a teal color, constitutes 6.0% of the net savings. The "Regional Average" scenario, depicted in a light orange hue, makes up 18.4% of the net savings. Following that, the "Combined Average" scenario, colored in light blue, represents 18.1% of the net savings. Lastly, the "National Average" scenario, shown in a light pink color, accounts for the largest share at 30.2% of the net savings. Each segment of the ring chart is labeled with the scenario name and the corresponding percentage value, making it easy to understand the contribution of each scenario to the overall net savings. The chart uses a soft, distinct color palette to differentiate between the scenarios, enhancing readability and visual appeal. The percentages are displayed in bold, black text within each segment, while the scenario labels are in a smaller, black font outside the ring. The chart provides a clear and concise visual summary of how net savings are distributed across different scenarios over the specified period.
